spec_match.rationale: The page now treats X25519Kyber768 as TLS/QUIC network-stack behavior and avoids claiming that JavaScript can detect the negotiated KEM. WebCrypto X25519 and Resource Timing nextHopProtocol are presented as adjacent observable surfaces only.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP exercised the repaired live WebCrypto key test and each scenario button. The controls update the key-test output and transport failure model in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index was updated to match the repaired concept: WebCrypto probes, Resource Timing boundary, and server/middlebox/policy/legacy fallback paths.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now includes explicit failure scenarios for classical-only servers, middlebox ClientHello drops, removed enterprise opt-out policy, and legacy TLS or client paths.

references.rationale: The concept page now links to chromestatus.com/feature/5257822742249472 plus the IETF draft, Resource Timing nextHopProtocol definition, and the feature conformance probe.

design_system.rationale: No raw hex colors were found in the concept CSS, and the new button background overrides pair background with color using shared design tokens.

summary: Repaired concept: removes the JavaScript KEM-detection overclaim, adds transport failure scenarios, and restores the required ChromeStatus/reference anchors. Pre-fix Chrome DevTools MCP evidence showed nextHopProtocol as http/1.1, no ChromeStatus link, one button, and no failure-mode control; post-fix Chrome DevTools MCP validation exercised the WebCrypto button, all scenario buttons, references, and the updated 8/8 conformance suite with clean console and network output.
